If this is the case, Molucella laevis is a plant species of the genus Molucella, which is a flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant Molucella laevis is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

The bioactive constituents of Molucella laevis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenes, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Molucella laevis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve stress.
